title: Clean Fuels Weekly Commentary: Q3 LCFS Data Drives LCFS Credit Prices Higher; Trading Volumes Decline for Week of Jan 26th
content:
This Weekâ€™s Highlights

(26th January 2026 â€“ 1st February 2026)
  • CARB Released Q3 2025 Credit Data, marking a significant rise in deficit generation and a drawdown of 1.87 MT credits from the net credit bank.
  • US Treasury Department releases proposed rule for 45Z tax credit, including some key clarifications on previous language.
  • A total of 403,183 credits were exchanged as Type 1 trades, a decrease of 44.92% week-over-week (WoW), while all non-zero trades also decreased 22.63% WoW and a total of 1,543,183 non-zero credits were traded in CA LCFS.
  • The weekly average CA LCFS credit price stood at $62.32/MT for Type 1 transfers, up by 6.00% WoW; prices of all non-zero transfers rose by 2.26%, price stood at $64.19/MT.
  • The average LCFS spot credit price rose by 2.82% WoW; on 3rd February, the spot price stood at $69.38/MT, and the benchmark price stood at $72.88/MT.
  • D3 RIN prices fell by 0.42% WoW, while D4, D5 and D6 RIN prices increased by 1.61%, 1.63% and 2.48% WoW respectively.

cCarbonâ€™s Market View

Last weekÂ on Jan 30th, CARB released its quarterly data forÂ Q3 2025, marking the first time deficits have outpaced credits since Q1 2021. The Q3 credit data has been awaited by the market since the LCFS rulemaking was finalized last year, and is the first quarter of data to reflect deficits generated under a more stringent CI trajectory. Total deficits generated in the quarter were 10.04 MT, compared with credit generation at 8.33 MT, implying a reduction of 1.87 MT from the existing LCFS credit bank. The Q3 data was well-received, with prices in the secondary market surging over the first few days of February. cCarbonâ€™s modeling had suggested a notable increase in deficit generation to begin in Q3 2025, but even our expectations were exceeded. Our projected deficit generation for Q3 was 9.1 MT, falling ~800,000 short of the actuals. On the other hand, Q3 also saw a greater decline in credit generation than our projected number of 9.47 MT.Â  The reduction in the net credit bank marks an inflection point for the LCFS market, indicating a shift towards higher prices after a 3.5 year period of relative stagnation. cCarbon's modeling had previously suggested that the bank drawdown was set to begin in Q4 2025, albeit at a more even rate, and credit generators will be pleased with the late holiday present.Â  Renewable Diesel Trends Drive LCFS Bank DrawdownÂ  A combination of lower RD volumes and an increased CI for fossil Diesel is the primary reason for Q3â€™s credit data, a sort of perfect storm in what has otherwise been a steadily increasing RD blend in the state. Given that increased clarity on RFS volume requirements emerged just around the beginning of Q3 2025, cCarbon expects RD blending to have recovered somewhat by Q4 2025 and a correspondingly smaller delta between credit and deficit generation in the program over the rest of 2025 and early 2026. OurÂ team will be working hard on model updates over the coming weeks, and we hope you will join us for tomorrowâ€™s Great ExpectationsÂ webinarÂ to discuss LCFS market outlook, as well as our view on other clean fuel standard markets such as the Canada CFR, OR CFP, WA CFS and BC LCFS.Â 

CA LCFS Weekly Price and Volume Trend

[caption id="attachment_612462" align="alignnone" width="709"] Table 1. Weekly average credit prices and weekly volumes, Source: California ARB[/caption] The average Type 1 transfer price increased by 6.00% WoW, rising from $58.79 to $62.32, while all non-zero transfer prices moved up from $62.77 to $64.19, a 2.26% WoW gain. Type 1 trades totaled 403,183 credits, reflecting a 44.92% WoW decrease and total non-zero transaction volumes fell by 22.63% WoW, reaching 1,543,183 credits for the week. [caption id="attachment_612463" align="alignnone" width="1300"] Fig 1. Weekly average credit prices and weekly volumes, Source: California ARB[/caption]

CA LCFS Daily Average Spot Price

[caption id="attachment_612464" align="alignnone" width="1300"] Fig 2. California LCFS Weekly average spot prices and benchmark (*CC.Info OTC: Numbers are an average of broker prices reported to CC.info)[/caption] Last week, the average LCFS spot credit prices rose by 2.82% WoW, reaching $63.78/MT. On 3rd February, the spot price stood at $69.38/MT. The benchmark price, representing the future price of contracts expiring at the end of 2026, stood at $72.88/MT on 3rd February.

LCFS Weekly Trade Frequency and Volume

[caption id="attachment_612465" align="alignnone" width="1300"] Fig 3. Trade frequency and volume, Source: California ARB[/caption] As per the data reported by CARB last week, a total of 83 trades took place in the market, including 9 high-volume transactions. The highest reported transfer price was $77.35 for a Type 2 transaction involving 500,000 credits. The lowest transfer price was $44.59 for a Type 2 transaction covering 10,000 credits. The largest trade by volume was a Type 2 transaction of 500,000 credits priced at $77.35, while the smallest was a Type 1 transfer of 690 credits priced at $54.50.

CA LCFS Monthly Volumes and Price Trend

[caption id="attachment_612466" align="alignnone" width="1300"] Fig 4. Total monthly volumes and associated prices, Source: California ARB[/caption] The average Type 1 spot price stood at $58.59 in January so far, rising from $53.08 in December. As of 31st January 2026, trade volumes for the month reached 8.07M.

CA LCFS Weekly Volume Price Trend

[caption id="attachment_612467" align="alignnone" width="1300"] Fig 5. Volume Price Trend indicator for the CA LCFS market, Source: California ARB[/caption] The Volume Price Trend Indicator (VPT) helps identify the parity between supply and demand for stock. The idea behind the indicator is to multiply the market volume of LCFS credits by the percentage change in its price. If the credit price declines, the value of the indicator falls. Across 26th January 2026 â€“ 1st February 2026, total VPT rose by 2.40% WoW, with a rise in all non-zero credit prices for the last week.

Treasury Releases Updated 45Z Tax Credit Proposal

The US Department of Treasury released their proposed rule for the 45Z tax credit, a long awaited guidance on how the final 45Z credit will be applied and who will be eligible to benefit. cCarbon staff will be digesting the proposal over the coming days so look for more detailed commentary soon!

Renewable Fuel Standard (RFS): RIN Weekly Price Trend

RINs are credits used for compliance and are the â€œcurrencyâ€ of the RFS program. Renewable fuel producers generate RINs, market participants trade RINs, and obligated parties obtain and then ultimately retire RINs for the compliance list. D3 RIN prices fell by 0.42% WoW, while D4, D5 and D6 RIN prices increased by 1.61%, 1.63% and 2.48% WoW respectively. D3: Cellulosic biofuel D4: Biomass-based diesel D5: Advanced biofuels (produced from any type of renewable biomass except corn starch ethanol) D6: Conventional renewable biofuel ethanol derived from corn starch, or any other qualifying renewable fuel

Upcoming DatesÂ 

Key Clean Fuels Market Developments This Week

  • RFA Applauds President Trumpâ€™s Call for Congressional Action on Year-Round E15: The Renewable Fuels Association applauded President Trump for strongly reaffirming his support for Americaâ€™s ethanol industry and calling on Congress to send a bill approving year-round E15 â€œto my desk very quickly.â€ Trump said he would sign the bill into law â€œwithout delay.â€
  • CF Industries, POET, and Major Agriculture Co-Operatives Launch Low-Carbon Fertilizer Pilot to Cut Ethanol Production Carbon Intensity: CF Industries Holdings, a leading global manufacturer of hydrogen and nitrogen products, and POET, the worldâ€™s largest producer of biofuels, have launched a pilot project with major agriculture co-operatives to jointly develop a low-carbon fertilizers supply chain. The goal of the pilot is to demonstrate how the use of low carbon nitrogen fertilizer can substantially reduce the carbon intensity of corn and enable the production of low carbon ethanol for use in motor fuel and export.
  • Arva Launches Audit-Ready 45Z Infrastructure to Help Biofuel Producers Maximize Credit Value: Arva announced the expansion of its biofuels offering to support biofuel producers as they operationalize the 45Z Clean Fuel Production Credit. As fuel value becomes more directly tied to feedstock Carbon Intensity (CI), producers face growing pressure to source sufficient volumes of low-CI feedstocks while ensuring claims are defensible under audits. Arva delivers the digital infrastructure, primary grower data, and compliance expertise required to maximize 45Z value while reducing audit risk.
  • 2025 Ethanol Exports Set a Second Consecutive Annual Record in Just 11 Months: According to data released by the Census Bureau, 2025 U.S. ethanol exports through November totaled 1.96 billion gallons, already surpassing annual shipments of 1.94 billion gallons in 2024, which had smashed the previous record. With one month of data to go, calendar year 2025 exports were on pace to exceed 2 billion gallons for the first time, which would represent 13 percent of U.S. ethanol production, also a record.
  • Washington SB 5932: Providing Certainty for the Development of Low-to-Zero Carbon Alternative Jet Fuel Production: Washington state is reinforcing its position as a global leader in the Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) market with the introduction of Senate Bill 5932. This legislation is the latest power move by the legislature to provide the gold standard of financial and regulatory certainty for the Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) industry. Breaking Status Update: The bill is moving at high speed. After being introduced on January 12, 2026, it has already advanced to a critical milestone. It is scheduled for an executive session in the Senate Committee on Environment, Energy & Technology on February 3, 2026, at 1:30 PM.
  • California Accelerates Work on EV Rules, Tax Credits that Trump Opposes, State's Top Air Regulator Says: California officials are meeting Detroit automakers this week to discuss the next phase of greenhouse gas regulations for cars and trucks, the state's top air regulator said. California is fighting in the courts and in the U.S. Congress against President Donald Trump's efforts to dismantle landmark federal vehicle emissions standards. Governor Gavin Newsom plans an announcement next week that will lay out details of a new $200 million electric vehicle incentive program that aims to help fill the gap left after Trumpâ€™s budget bill killed federal tax credits for new electric cars last year, Lauren Sanchez, chair of the California Air Resources Board, told Reuters in an interview
  • RFA Thanks Trump Administration for Guatemala Agreement Boosting Ethanol Exports: The Renewable Fuels Association thanked President Donald Trump and U.S. Trade Representative, Jamieson Greer, for securing reciprocal trade agreement with Guatemala that contains a requirement for 10 percent ethanol blends for on-road gasoline (E10), and a purchase commitment for at least 50 million gallons of American-made ethanol annually. The agreement also includes zero tariffs for U.S. agricultural products.
  • Broad Coalition of Farm and Fuel Leaders Rally Behind Immediate E15 Fix: A broad coalition of trade groups representing ethanol producers, petroleum refiners, farmers, and retailers sent a letter to the co-chairs of the new E15 Rural Domestic Energy Council calling for swift action to deliver lower prices for consumers and a stable, efficient fuels marketplace. The letter outlines recommendations for consensus legislation to permit year-round, nationwide sales of E15 and improve long-term policy certainty across the transportation fuel sector.
